idea-plugin.name: Simplified Annotations
idea-plugin.description:
Three annotation families for Java with companion IDE tooling: @ResourcePath (static resource-path validation), @XContract (superset of JetBrains @Contract), and @ClassBuilder (Lombok-@Builder superset with annotation-processor code generation).

@ClassBuilder

  • Annotation processor generates a sibling <TypeName>Builder.java for every class, record, or interface annotated with @ClassBuilder. Interfaces also get a matching <Name>Impl concrete class.
  • Full Lombok @Builder parity: configurable method names (builderMethodName, buildMethodName, fromMethodName, toBuilderMethodName), method prefix, access level, custom factory method, exclusions.
  • Setter-shape extras that Lombok doesn't cover: dual Optional<T> setters, zero-arg + typed boolean pairs with @Negate, @PrintFormat string overloads via @Formattable, @Singular add/put/varargs/clear for collections and maps, defensive-copy from(T), and a mutate() instance shorthand.
  • @BuilderDefault copies the field's declared initializer into the builder so unset fields start at the intended value (UUID.randomUUID(), sensible strings, pre-populated collections) instead of the JVM default. @ObtainVia redirects from(T) to a non-standard accessor - an instance method, a static helper, or a different field - when the target type doesn't expose a matching getter.
  • @BuildFlag runtime validator enforces nonNull / notEmpty / group mutual-requirement / regex pattern / length limit in the generated build(). Zero external dependencies.
  • @XContract annotations are emitted on every generated method so IntelliJ data-flow analysis understands fresh-object and this-return shapes.
  • IDE inspections: Bootstrap-methods missing (ERROR-level) with a quick-fix that inserts builder() / from(T) / mutate() all at once; Field annotation misuse catches mistakes like @Formattable on a non-String field.

@ResourcePath

  • Resource Path inspection - verifies that every annotated field, parameter, or method return resolves to a file that exists in the project's content source roots (or in user-configured additional roots). Supports an optional base attribute that prefixes the validated path.
  • Resource Path Base-Prefix Usage inspection - catches the common runtime mistake of passing a @ResourcePath(base="X") parameter raw into a resource-loading call (Class.getResourceAsStream, new File(...), etc.) without prepending X/. Offers a quick-fix. Also flags base mismatches across method boundaries.
  • Value resolution follows literals, concatenation, final/static/enum fields, local variables, method-call return values (recursively), and enum-constructor bindings, with bounded recursion and result size.
  • Settings: custom resource-root paths, glob-based file exclusions, separate severity for invalid-base errors.

@XContract

  • Superset of JetBrains @Contract with a richer grammar: relational comparisons, && / || with grouping, named-parameter references, instanceof conditions, typed throws returns, chained comparisons (0 <= index < size()), and full pure / mutates support.
  • A built-in InferredAnnotationProvider synthesises an equivalent @Contract for every @XContract method, so IntelliJ's data-flow analysis works from a single annotation.
  • XContract inspection validates contract syntax, parameter references, type references, mutates tokens, and flags overrides that weaken a super method's contract.
  • XContract Violation at Call Site inspection warns when a call's literal arguments deterministically trigger a fail or throws clause.
